Document:
# Species
NanoTrasen employs a variety of sapient Species in this sector of space.
All Species are [bold][color=#ff0]equally capable[/color][/bold] of work on the station, but differences in physiology make for certain [bold]benefits[/bold], [bold]drawbacks[/bold], and [bold]special considerations[/bold] crew members have to keep in mind.
Species have differences in [bold]body temperature[/bold], [bold]dietary needs[/bold], and [bold]unique features[/bold]; Species may have [bold]active abilities[/bold] or [bold]passive traits[/bold] that impact their life and work.
The following pages will detail individual Species and their physiology, serving as a [bold][color=#ff0]quick reference[/color][/bold] for the most impactful variables differrent between them. Also listed are typical [bold]naming conventions[/bold] Species usually follow, though a person might take or be given a different name in their lifetime, not necessarily matching their own or other Species' conventions.
